goal: Run a complete N=5 teleoperated demonstration RECORDING session for GR00T fine-tuning: (1) check_teleop_hardware(device='spacemouse') probes the input device transport and latency budget; (2) configure_teleop_mapping wires SpaceMouse axes to Franka joints with calibrated gains; (3) start_teleop_session opens the WebSocket bridge and physics callback; (4) record_teleop_demo runs 5 times to capture 5 HDF5 episodes of pick-cube → place-in-bin at 30 Hz (joint pos/vel + EE pose per timestep); (5) validate_teleop_demo gates each HDF5 file against the robomimic schema (action shape, obs keys, NaN check); (6) summarize_teleop_session reports per-demo duration, joint range, and total session statistics; (7) stop_teleop_session zeros velocities and finalises the WebSocket. SUCCESS criterion: 5 HDF5 files exist under workspace/demos/groot_recordings/ AND every file passes validate_teleop_demo AND summarize_teleop_session returns non-zero demo_count. This is the RECORDING-TIME canonical — it produces the raw HDF5 corpus that CP-NEW-groot-finetune-n10-demos later consumes for LoRA fine-tuning, and that CP-NEW-groot-export-dataset-hdf5 packages for downstream training. Distinct from CP-NEW-teaching-mode-pose-replay (programmatic waypoint capture, no human-in-the-loop). Scene complexity is deliberately minimal (one cube, one bin, Franka on a table) — the canonical exercises the recording infrastructure, not the task difficulty.
thoughts: Pattern novelty: no existing canonical covers the RECORDING-TIME teleop session lifecycle. CP-NEW-teaching-mode-pose-replay does drag-target waypoint capture (no hardware, no HDF5 output). CP-NEW-groot-finetune-n10-demos and CP-NEW-groot-export-dataset-hdf5 CONSUME demo HDF5 — this canonical PRODUCES them.

Scene design: minimal pick-place — Franka on table, single source cube, single destination bin. The complexity is in the recording infrastructure (hardware probe → mapping → session → N demos → validate → summarise → stop), not the task. Five identical pick-place episodes give the downstream fine-tuner a small but structurally-valid corpus.

Hardware probe FIRST. check_teleop_hardware(device='spacemouse') runs before start_teleop_session so the LLM can detect 'device not connected' and abort or fall back to keyboard. The handler returns transport (USB-HID for SpaceMouse, WebXR for Quest 3, CloudXR for Vision Pro) plus a known-limitations list — this is the natural decision point for graceful degradation.

Mapping BEFORE session start. configure_teleop_mapping installs the axis → joint table on the robot articulation. For SpaceMouse 6-DoF: (translate_x, translate_y, translate_z, rotate_x, rotate_y, rotate_z) → end-effector Cartesian deltas resolved via IK to all 7 panda joints. The handler accepts a flat joint_names list and gains dict; gains are conservative (position=400, velocity=40) to keep the first-time operator from oscillating the arm.

Five demos via a Python for-loop. record_teleop_demo(output_path=demos/groot_recordings/demo_{i:03d}.hdf5, robot_path, frequency_hz=30) writes one HDF5 per call. Between recordings we step physics 60 frames to let the cube re-settle if it was moved by the operator. In direct-eval / no-hardware mode the handler still creates the HDF5 file (with zero-length action timeseries) so the structural gate (file exists, schema valid) still passes.

validate_teleop_demo per-file. The handler asserts: 'actions' dataset present + correct shape (T, action_dim); 'obs' group present with required keys (joint_pos, joint_vel, ee_pose, at least 1 camera stream); no NaN/Inf in actions. We gate on every-file-passes — one bad HDF5 fails the canonical. In no-hardware mode the zero-length file still passes structural checks (T=0 is valid robomimic shape).

summarize_teleop_session per-file gives per-demo duration + joint range + velocity histogram. We aggregate these into a session-level dict and persist to workspace/demos/groot_recordings/session_summary.json. Total demo_count must equal 5.

stop_teleop_session takes no arguments. The handler zeros joint velocities (safety), tears down the physics callback, closes the WebSocket, and finalises any in-flight HDF5 file. Must be called even if record_teleop_demo errored mid-loop — otherwise the next session's start_teleop_session inherits stale state.

motion_controllers is empty by design. Teleop is human-in-the-loop — there is no planner. direct_joint is the closest descriptor but it's tagged 'untested' because the canonical doesn't itself drive joint targets; the operator does. cuRobo/RMPflow/MoveIt2 are explicitly N/A for raw recording (they would be relevant during replay or training-time policy execution).

pattern_hint='other' — the canonical doesn't deliver an object (no physics gate), doesn't train a policy, doesn't sort or navigate. It's a data-collection session. The structural_tags isaac:groot.demo_record + isaac:teleop.recording_session + isaac:dataset.foundation_model_format are the discriminating retrieval signal.

HARD BLOCKER for function-gate: SpaceMouse not present on dev box. The canonical falls back to keyboard (handler-provided) which still produces a valid HDF5 schema but with low operator skill — useful for plumbing tests but not for training data. Genuine function-gate requires (a) real SpaceMouse, (b) a human operator, (c) a quiet 5-10 min session. This is honest in verified_status.
code:
import json
import os
import time

# ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEMOS_DIR = "workspace/demos/groot_recordings"
ROBOT_PATH = "/World/Franka"
N_DEMOS = 5
FREQUENCY_HZ = 30
DEVICE = "spacemouse"
SESSION_SUMMARY_FILE = os.path.join(DEMOS_DIR, "session_summary.json")
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/groot_teleop_demo_record_result.json"

os.makedirs(DEMOS_DIR, exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs("/tmp", exist_ok=True)

# ── Scene skeleton: light + ground + table + cube + bin ──────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.5, 0.4, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/SourceCube", prim_type="Cube", position=[0.40, 0.20, 0.78], scale=[0.04, 0.04, 0.04])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/SourceCube", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/SourceCube", schema_name="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Bin", prim_type="Cube", position=[0.00, -0.30, 0.78], scale=[0.10, 0.10, 0.02])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Bin", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics, deterministic ─────────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Franka Panda robot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

sim_control(action="play")
sim_control(action="step", step_count=30)

# ── Step 1: Probe teleop hardware ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Required: device (str). Returns transport, latency budget, limitations.
hw_report = check_teleop_hardware(device=DEVICE)
print("[HW]", hw_report)

# If the device is not present, the handler returns a dict with 'supported': False
# or transport=='unsupported'. We log and continue — record_teleop_demo will still
# create structurally-valid (zero-length) HDF5 files in fallback mode.
hw_ok = isinstance(hw_report, dict) and hw_report.get("supported", True) is not False
if not hw_ok:
    print(f"[WARN] {DEVICE} not supported on this host; falling back to keyboard-emulated session")

# ── Step 2: Configure SpaceMouse → Franka joint mapping ──────────────────────
# Required: robot_path. Optional: device_axes, joint_names, gains.
FRANKA_JOINTS = [
    "panda_joint1", "panda_joint2", "panda_joint3",
    "panda_joint4", "panda_joint5", "panda_joint6", "panda_joint7",
]
SPACEMOUSE_AXES = [
    "translate_x", "translate_y", "translate_z",
    "rotate_x", "rotate_y", "rotate_z",
]
configure_teleop_mapping(
    robot_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    device_axes=SPACEMOUSE_AXES,
    joint_names=FRANKA_JOINTS,
    gains={"position": 400, "velocity": 40},
)

# ── Step 3: Start teleop session ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Required: robot_path. Optional: input_device, stream_quality.
start_teleop_session(
    robot_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    input_device=DEVICE,
    stream_quality="medium",
)

# ── Step 4: Record N=5 demonstrations ────────────────────────────────────────
# Required: output_path (str), robot_path (str). Optional: frequency_hz (int).
recorded_files = []
for i in range(N_DEMOS):
    demo_path = os.path.join(DEMOS_DIR, f"demo_{i:03d}.hdf5")
    print(f"[RECORD] Demo {i+1}/{N_DEMOS} → {demo_path}")
    try:
        rec_result = record_teleop_demo(
            output_path=demo_path,
            robot_path=ROBOT_PATH,
            frequency_hz=FREQUENCY_HZ,
        )
        print(f"[RECORD] result: {rec_result}")
        # Settle physics between demos so the next demo starts from a clean cube pose
        sim_control(action="step", step_count=60)
        if os.path.exists(demo_path):
            recorded_files.append(demo_path)
        else:
            print(f"[WARN] HDF5 not written: {demo_path}")
    except Exception as exc:
        print(f"[ERR] record_teleop_demo failed at demo {i}: {exc}")

print(f"[RECORD] {len(recorded_files)}/{N_DEMOS} demos written")

# ── Step 5: Validate every HDF5 against robomimic schema ─────────────────────
# Required: hdf5_path. Asserts action shape, obs keys, no NaN/Inf.
validation_results = {}
all_valid = True
for demo_path in recorded_files:
    try:
        validation = validate_teleop_demo(hdf5_path=demo_path)
        validation_results[demo_path] = validation
        if isinstance(validation, dict) and validation.get("valid", True) is False:
            all_valid = False
            print(f"[WARN] {demo_path} failed schema validation: {validation.get('errors', [])}")
    except Exception as exc:
        all_valid = False
        validation_results[demo_path] = {"valid": False, "error": str(exc)}
        print(f"[ERR] validate_teleop_demo raised on {demo_path}: {exc}")

# ── Step 6: Summarise each demo and aggregate session statistics ─────────────
# Required: hdf5_path. Optional: fps (defaults to file attr or 30).
per_demo_summaries = {}
for demo_path in recorded_files:
    try:
        summary = summarize_teleop_session(hdf5_path=demo_path, fps=FREQUENCY_HZ)
        per_demo_summaries[demo_path] = summary
    except Exception as exc:
        per_demo_summaries[demo_path] = {"error": str(exc)}

# Session-level aggregate
session_summary = {
    "demo_count": len(recorded_files),
    "target_demo_count": N_DEMOS,
    "all_valid": all_valid,
    "hardware_supported": hw_ok,
    "device": DEVICE,
    "frequency_hz": FREQUENCY_HZ,
    "per_demo_summaries": per_demo_summaries,
    "validation_results": validation_results,
}
with open(SESSION_SUMMARY_FILE, "w") as f:
    json.dump(session_summary, f, indent=2, default=str)
print(f"[SUMMARY] Session summary written to {SESSION_SUMMARY_FILE}")

# ── Step 7: Stop teleop session (always — even after errors) ─────────────────
# No args — zeros joint velocities, closes WebSocket, finalises any in-flight HDF5.
try:
    stop_teleop_session()
    print("[STOP] Teleop session stopped cleanly")
except Exception as exc:
    print(f"[WARN] stop_teleop_session raised: {exc}")

sim_control(action="stop")

# ── Step 8: Final pass/fail gate ─────────────────────────────────────────────
files_on_disk = sum(1 for p in recorded_files if os.path.exists(p))
session_ok = (
    files_on_disk == N_DEMOS
    and all_valid
    and session_summary["demo_count"] == N_DEMOS
)

result_payload = {
    "task_id": "CP-NEW-groot-teleop-demo-record",
    "session_ok": session_ok,
    "files_on_disk": files_on_disk,
    "target_demo_count": N_DEMOS,
    "all_valid": all_valid,
    "hardware_supported": hw_ok,
    "session_summary_file": SESSION_SUMMARY_FILE,
    "recorded_files": recorded_files,
}
with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
    json.dump(result_payload, f, indent=2)

if session_ok:
    print(f"[OK] Teleop recording session complete: {N_DEMOS} valid HDF5 demos at {DEMOS_DIR}")
else:
    print(
        f"[WARN] Session partial: {files_on_disk}/{N_DEMOS} files on disk, "
        f"all_valid={all_valid}. Check {SESSION_SUMMARY_FILE} for per-demo issues."
    )

get_console_errors(last_n=10, min_level="error")

assert os.path.exists(RESULT_FILE), "Result file must exist regardless of session_ok"
failure_modes: check_teleop_hardware returns 'supported': False on hosts without the named device — fall back to keyboard (handler default) or abort cleanly; do NOT proceed to record_teleop_demo with an unsupported device because joint commands will be zero

start_teleop_session opens a WebSocket on port 8765 by default — if another teleop session is already active (from a prior crashed run that did not call stop_teleop_session), the bind will fail; call stop_teleop_session in a try/except cleanup before starting

record_teleop_demo writes HDF5 incrementally — if the operator (or test runner) terminates the process mid-recording, the file is partially written and validate_teleop_demo will report a truncated 'actions' dataset; the canonical's try/except logs but does not retry

DEMOS_DIR write permission: workspace/ may be on a read-only mount in containerised CI; the canonical creates the directory with os.makedirs(exist_ok=True) but the write itself can still fail — use os.access(DEMOS_DIR, os.W_OK) gate if running in CI

frequency_hz=30 is the default for robomimic-compatible recording; lowering to 10 Hz produces visibly jerky replay and may break downstream policies that assume 30 Hz; raising above 60 Hz inflates file size and may exceed disk write bandwidth on slow storage

Camera stream sync drift: the robomimic schema expects camera frames per-timestep; if the SDG render product runs at a different FPS than frequency_hz, the obs dict will have fewer or more camera entries than action steps and validate_teleop_demo will fail with an action/obs length mismatch

GR00T format spec change: NVIDIA may bump the LeRobot v2 / robomimic dataset schema between Isaac Sim releases; validate_teleop_demo against the current handler version is the canary — pin Isaac Sim version in extension_notes if a regression is observed

stop_teleop_session must be called even if record_teleop_demo errored — failure to clean up leaks the physics callback and the next session start will see stale joint commands; the canonical wraps stop_teleop_session in try/except for this reason

SUCCESS criterion requires ALL of: files_on_disk == 5, all validate_teleop_demo pass, session_summary.demo_count == 5; if any one fails, session_ok is False and the canonical fails the gate honestly (result file still written for inspection)
extension_notes: First teleop-recording canonical. Backlog source: groot-teleop-demo-record-001 (priority_tier=1, complexity=medium, asset_dependencies=[Franka, SpaceMouse], blockers=[hardware_spacemouse], motion_controller_preference=direct_joint, pattern_hint=finetune). Distinct from: CP-NEW-teaching-mode-pose-replay (programmatic waypoint capture, no hardware, no HDF5); CP-NEW-groot-finetune-n10-demos (CONSUMES this canonical's HDF5 output for LoRA fine-tuning); CP-NEW-groot-export-dataset-hdf5 (packages recorded HDF5 for downstream training). This canonical produces the RAW corpus the others depend on. Recording-time canonical = no policy, no planner, no model weights, no GPU VRAM requirement (Franka + 5 short HDF5 files). Hard-blocked at function-gate by SpaceMouse hardware + human operator; structural form-gate passes with fallback keyboard.

input_device.note: Hardware probed via check_teleop_hardware; keyboard is the always-available fallback

recording_output.note: Number of HDF5 files written to recording_output.path (one per demo)

diagnose_args.success_condition: files_on_disk == 5 AND all_valid == True AND demo_count == 5

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-A111; form-gate-schema ✓; function-gate BLOCKED (requires SpaceMouse hardware + human operator for genuine demos; fallback keyboard path produces structurally-valid but operator-empty HDF5 files)
